message:
style: normalize rustfmt formatting across fp-expr, fp-frame, fp-groupby, fp-io, fp-join

Comprehensive rustfmt pass to fix formatting drift accumulated across
multiple feature additions. No logic changes — purely whitespace and
line-break normalization to pass cargo fmt --check.

fp-expr:
- Collapse single-line error return in parse_atom
- Join query_str call onto single line in test

fp-frame (bulk of changes, ~4000 lines reformatted):
- Collapse short function arguments (from_values, from_dict_with_index,
  Self::new) from multi-line to single-line where they fit within the
  line limit
- Reflow long method chains (filter, map, collect) to standard rustfmt
  indentation style
- Collapse short if/else blocks to single-line form
- Reflow function signatures (where_cond, mask) to single-line
- Normalize match arm formatting for short expressions
- Reflow long format!() and assert!() macro arguments
- Standardize closure formatting in iterator chains
- Break long lines for chained method calls (.iter().filter().count())
- Normalize vec![] literal formatting in tests
- Reflow DataFrame::from_dict constructor calls in tests

fp-groupby:
- Expand inline vec![...] arguments in test data to one-element-per-line
- Collapse short two-element vec![] to single line
- Join short groupby_count/groupby_size result bindings

fp-io:
- Reorder use imports alphabetically (RecordBatch, StringArray,
  StringBuilder; ArrowWriter before arrow_reader)
- Break long assert_eq!() lines in parquet round-trip tests
- Collapse short DataFrame::new_with_column_order call

fp-join:
- Expand inline vec![] tuple arguments in merge_asof test data
  (left/right DataFrames) to structured multi-line format matching
  rustfmt's nested tuple+vec indentation rules

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -3026,13 +3026,24 @@ mod tests {
         let left = fp_frame::DataFrame::from_dict(
             &["time", "price"],
             vec![
-                ("time", vec![Scalar::Int64(1), Scalar::Int64(3), Scalar::Int64(5), Scalar::Int64(7)]),
-                ("price", vec![
-                    Scalar::Float64(100.0),
-                    Scalar::Float64(101.0),
-                    Scalar::Float64(102.0),
-                    Scalar::Float64(103.0),
-                ]),
+                (
+                    "time",
+                    vec![
+                        Scalar::Int64(1),
+                        Scalar::Int64(3),
+                        Scalar::Int64(5),
+                        Scalar::Int64(7),
+                    ],
+                ),
+                (
+                    "price",
+                    vec![
+                        Scalar::Float64(100.0),
+                        Scalar::Float64(101.0),
+                        Scalar::Float64(102.0),
+                        Scalar::Float64(103.0),
+                    ],
+                ),
             ],
         )
         .unwrap();
@@ -3041,12 +3052,18 @@ mod tests {
         let right = fp_frame::DataFrame::from_dict(
             &["time", "bid"],
             vec![
-                ("time", vec![Scalar::Int64(2), Scalar::Int64(4), Scalar::Int64(6)]),
-                ("bid", vec![
-                    Scalar::Float64(99.5),
-                    Scalar::Float64(100.5),
-                    Scalar::Float64(101.5),
-                ]),
+                (
+                    "time",
+                    vec![Scalar::Int64(2), Scalar::Int64(4), Scalar::Int64(6)],
+                ),
+                (
+                    "bid",
+                    vec![
+                        Scalar::Float64(99.5),
+                        Scalar::Float64(100.5),
+                        Scalar::Float64(101.5),
+                    ],
+                ),
             ],
         )
         .unwrap();
@@ -3109,12 +3126,18 @@ mod tests {
         let right = fp_frame::DataFrame::from_dict(
             &["time", "ref_val"],
             vec![
-                ("time", vec![Scalar::Int64(1), Scalar::Int64(5), Scalar::Int64(10)]),
-                ("ref_val", vec![
-                    Scalar::Float64(100.0),
-                    Scalar::Float64(500.0),
-                    Scalar::Float64(1000.0),
-                ]),
+                (
+                    "time",
+                    vec![Scalar::Int64(1), Scalar::Int64(5), Scalar::Int64(10)],
+                ),
+                (
+                    "ref_val",
+                    vec![
+                        Scalar::Float64(100.0),
+                        Scalar::Float64(500.0),
+                        Scalar::Float64(1000.0),
+                    ],
+                ),
             ],
         )
         .unwrap();
